item-handbooktext-speedmeat1-raw: Tier I speed: looks like a cooked fillet, but is still raw and spoils like red meat. When it rots, it becomes rot like other raw meats. Cook on a fire pit or in a forge, then eat the cooked piece once to earn the permanent Meat Speed trait. It counts as red-meat–style pot pie filling; in a cooking pot it can make stinkbait (red meat) the same as normal redmeat (see Ingredient for). Craft (3×3, middle column only, top to bottom): any dead butterfly, raw redmeat or raw bushmeat, and a catmint flower in the hand.
item-handbooktext-speedmeat1-cooked: 280 protein satiety, like cooked redmeat. The first one you eat grants a permanent Meat Speed bonus; later portions still feed you, but the charm does not stack. Combine with a Training Manual: Speed II (tier II) for more movement—each tier is separate.
game:traitdesc-traitbuffs-meatspeed: Your legs remember the first spiced meal. (+100% walk speed, +100% sprint speed) (Speed Tier I)
traitdesc-traitbuffs-meatspeed: Your legs remember the first spiced meal. (+100% walk speed, +100% sprint speed) (Speed Tier I)
traitbuffs:traitdesc-traitbuffs-meatspee: Your legs remember the first spiced meal. (+100% walk speed, +100% sprint speed) (Speed Tier I)
item-handbooktext-trainingmanual-speed:
Read once, not a journal. This volume records movement drills. rightmouse to study it: the first time you gain a permanent speed increase; after that, you have nothing left to learn from it. It is not a blank book—ink and quill will not open it for writing.

How to craft (3×3)
Left column, top to bottom: any normal leather, 8 parchment, any normal leather. Middle column, top to bottom: ink and quill (returned when you craft, not consumed), one cinnabar powder, empty. Right column: leave empty.
item-handbooktext-granolabar-speed:
Tier III speed catalyst. Eat this charred sunflower-like ration to fill yourself and attempt to unlock Speed Bar. The bar can only awaken once your body already holds Meat Speed (Tier I) and Speed Training (Tier II).

Craft (3×3)
Top row: Training Manual: Speed II (returned, not consumed), cooked Speedmeat I, any fruit ×4.
Middle row: any seeds ×4, steel ingot, lump of raw fat ×2.
Bottom row: powdered lapis lazuli, fired ceramic bowl with 0.25L honey, any flour ×4.
game:traitdesc-traitbuffs-speedtraining: Movement drills increase your walking and running speed permanently. (+100% walk speed, +100% sprint speed) (Speed Tier II)
traitdesc-traitbuffs-speedtraining: Movement drills increase your walking and running speed permanently. (+100% walk speed, +100% sprint speed) (Speed Tier II)
traitbuffs:traitdesc-traitbuffs-speedtra: Movement drills increase your walking and running speed permanently. (+100% walk speed, +100% sprint speed) (Speed Tier II)
game:traitdesc-traitbuffs-speedbar: A dense bar of fuel teaches your body one final sprint cadence. (+100% walk speed, +100% sprint speed) (Speed Tier III)
traitdesc-traitbuffs-speedbar: A dense bar of fuel teaches your body one final sprint cadence. (+100% walk speed, +100% sprint speed) (Speed Tier III)
traitbuffs:traitdesc-traitbuffs-speedbar: A dense bar of fuel teaches your body one final sprint cadence. (+100% walk speed, +100% sprint speed) (Speed Tier III)
item-handbooktext-healthmeat1-raw: Tier I vitality: looks like a cooked fillet, but is still raw and spoils like red meat. When it rots, it becomes rot like other raw meats. Cook on a fire pit or in a forge, then eat the cooked piece once for a permanent Meat Heart bonus (+2 max health, +healing effectiveness) (Health Tier I). Same pie and stinkbait roles as speedmeat. Craft (3×3, middle column only, top to bottom): any dead butterfly, raw redmeat or raw bushmeat, and a mugwort flower in the hand.
item-handbooktext-healthmeat1-cooked: 280 protein satiety, like cooked redmeat. The first one you eat grants Meat Heart (+2 max health, +healing effectiveness) (Health Tier I); later portions still feed you, but the charm does not stack. Combine with Training Manual: Health II for Tier II.
item-handbooktext-trainingmanual-health:
Read once, not a journal. This volume records conditioning for endurance and recovery. rightmouse to study it: the first time you gain a permanent health bonus; after that, you have nothing left to learn from it. Requires Health Tier I (Meat Heart).

How to craft (3×3)
Same layout as the Speed manual, but the reagent is powdered iron instead of cinnabar.
item-handbooktext-granolabar-health:
Tier III health catalyst. Eat this charred sunflower-like ration to unlock Health Bar (+4 max health, +healing effectiveness) (Health Tier III) only if you already have Meat Heart (Tier I) and Health Training (Tier II).

Craft (3×3)
Top row: Training Manual: Health II (returned, not consumed), cooked Healthmeat I, any fruit ×4.
Middle row: any seeds ×4, steel ingot, lump of raw fat ×2.
Bottom row: powdered lapis lazuli, fired ceramic bowl with 0.25L honey, any flour ×4.
game:traitdesc-traitbuffs-meathp: Rich cuts taught your body to keep a little more life in reserve. (+2 max health, +4% healing effectiveness) (Health Tier I)
traitdesc-traitbuffs-meathp: Rich cuts taught your body to keep a little more life in reserve. (+2 max health, +4% healing effectiveness) (Health Tier I)
traitbuffs:traitdesc-traitbuffs-meathp: Rich cuts taught your body to keep a little more life in reserve. (+2 max health, +4% healing effectiveness) (Health Tier I)
game:traitdesc-traitbuffs-healthtraining: Manual drills widen your reserves and steady mending. (+2 max health, +4% healing effectiveness) (Health Tier II)
traitdesc-traitbuffs-healthtraining: Manual drills widen your reserves and steady mending. (+2 max health, +4% healing effectiveness) (Health Tier II)
traitbuffs:traitdesc-traitbuffs-healthtr: Manual drills widen your reserves and steady mending. (+2 max health, +4% healing effectiveness) (Health Tier II)
game:traitdesc-traitbuffs-healthbar: A dense ration seals what the manual started. (+4 max health, +8% healing effectiveness) (Health Tier III)

traitbuffs:gui-additional-traits-empty: No Trait Buffs traits yet. Use this mod's foods, manuals, and rations to earn entries. The class list at the top is from character creation only.
